ucs.title: [LLM] GOLD SOUTHFIELD / Pinchy Spider — REvil/Sodinokibi pre-encryption recovery-inhibit ritual (vssadmin + bcdedit + wbadmin chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the very specific recovery-inhibit command sequence hard-coded into REvil/Sodinokibi (operated by GOLD SOUTHFIELD a.k.a. Pinchy Spider) — vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet, bcdedit /set {default} bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures + recoveryenabled No, and wbadmin delete catalog -quiet executed within minutes of each other on the same host. This ordered triple is REvil's signature pre-encryption ritual and is rarely seen together outside of ransomware staging.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as binaries dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_inhibitors from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND ((Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*" AND Processes.process="*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" AND Processes.process="*Delete*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where distinct_inhibitors>=2 | sort 0 -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// REvil / Sodinokibi recovery-inhibit chain (GOLD SOUTHFIELD)
let WindowMin = 10m;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                          // skip machine accounts
| where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
     or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled","bootstatuspolicy"))
     or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"   and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject","Get-WmiObject") and ProcessCommandLine has "Shadowcopy")
| summarize
    InhibitorBins   = dcount(FileName),
    Commands        = make_set(FileName, 16),
    SampleCmdLines  = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 16),
    Parents         = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 8),
    FirstSeen       = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen        = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, WindowMin)
| where InhibitorBins >= 2                                  // 2+ distinct recovery-inhibit binaries in 10m = REvil ritual
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// REvil / Sodinokibi recovery-inhibit chain (GOLD SOUTHFIELD) — Sentinel SecurityEvent (EID 4688)
let WindowMin = 10m;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe"  and CommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled","bootstatuspolicy"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wbadmin.exe"  and CommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe"     and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
     or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "Shadowcopy" and CommandLine has_any ("Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject","Get-WmiObject"))
| extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| summarize
    InhibitorBins  = dcount(BinName),
    Commands       = make_set(BinName, 16),
    SampleCmdLines = make_set(CommandLine, 16),
    Parents        = make_set(ParentProcessName, 8),
    FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMin)
| where InhibitorBins >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: REvil/Sodinokibi Recovery-Inhibit Ritual (GOLD SOUTHFIELD / Pinchy Spider)
id: 7a4b1c5d-1d2e-4a4b-9b8e-revil-recovery-chain
status: experimental
description: Detects the canonical REvil/Sodinokibi pre-encryption recovery-inhibit command set (vssadmin/bcdedit/wbadmin/wmic shadowcopy) hard-coded into the REvil binary operated by GOLD SOUTHFIELD.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0115/
  - https://www.secureworks.com/research/threat-profiles/gold-southfield
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa20-126a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.g0115
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  vssadmin:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  bcdedit:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'recoveryenabled'
      - 'bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures'
  wbadmin:
    Image|endswith: '\wbadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'catalog'
  wmic_shadow:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  ps_shadow:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Win32_Shadowcopy'
  condition: 1 of vssadmin or bcdedit or wbadmin or wmic_shadow or ps_shadow
falsepositives:
  - Backup software lifecycle scripts (rare for vssadmin delete shadows)
  - Sysadmin manual recovery-config changes (correlate by user + window)
level: high
ucs.rationale: GOLD SOUTHFIELD's REvil/Sodinokibi binary is documented (Secureworks, Microsoft, CISA AA20-126A, Sophos) to ALWAYS execute this exact recovery-inhibit triple before encryption. The detection requires ≥2 distinct inhibitor binaries within 10 minutes on the same host — that combination is essentially never legitimate and is highly characteristic of REvil/Sodinokibi staging (also seen in the Travelex
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] GOLD SOUTHFIELD — Kaseya VSA-style supply-chain RMM abuse: Kaseya/RMM agent spawning certutil -decode + DLL-sideload of MsMpEng.exe + mpsvc.
ucs.description: Hunts the precise Kaseya VSA supply-chain deployment chain GOLD SOUTHFIELD used in July 2021 (and the broader trusted-relationship/MSP-tooling tradecraft Pinchy Spider continues to favour): the Kaseya AgentMon.exe (or a ConnectWise/N-able RMM agent) spawning cmd.exe to disable Defender via Set-MpPreference, copy certutil.exe to C:\Windows\cert.exe, base64-decode an agent.crt payload from C:\kworking\, then sideload mpsvc.dll into a renamed copy of MsMpEng.exe (Windows Defender's signed binary) to load REvil via T1574.002.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as procs values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("agentmon.exe","kaseya.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","AeXNSAgent.exe","winagent.exe") OR Processes.parent_process IN ("*\\Kaseya\\*","*\\ConnectWise\\*","*\\N-able\\*","*\\SolarWinds\\MspAgent\\*")) AND (Processes.process="*kworking*" OR Processes.process="*agent.crt*" OR Processes.process="*certutil*-decode*" OR Processes.process="*MsMpEng.exe*" OR Processes.process="*mpsvc.dll*" OR Processes.process="*Set-MpPreference*-Disable*" OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*ping*127.0.0.1*-n*" AND Processes.process="*copy*certutil*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | sort 0 -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Kaseya VSA-style supply-chain REvil drop (GOLD SOUTHFIELD)
let RmmAgents = dynamic(["agentmon.exe","kaseya.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","aexnsagent.exe","winagent.exe","saazapsc.exe"]);
let RmmPaths  = dynamic([@"\Kaseya\", @"\ConnectWise\", @"\N-able\", @"\SolarWinds\MspAgent\", @"\Datto\"]);
let KaseyaIOCs = dynamic(["kworking","agent.crt","mpsvc.dll"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (RmmAgents)
     or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (RmmPaths)
| where
      ProcessCommandLine has_any (KaseyaIOCs)
   or (FileName =~ "certutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "-decode")
   or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "Set-MpPreference" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-DisableRealtimeMonitoring","-DisableIOAVProtection","-DisableBehaviorMonitoring","-DisableScriptScanning"))
   or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "copy" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("certutil","\\Windows\\"))
   or (ProcessCommandLine has "MsMpEng.exe" and ProcessCommandLine !contains @"\Windows Defender\")    // legitimate MsMpEng lives under Defender platform; copies elsewhere are suspicious
   or (FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files\Windows Defender\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Platform\" and FileName =~ "MsMpEng.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentRMM    = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildBin     = FileName,
          ChildPath    = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Kaseya VSA-style supply-chain REvil drop (GOLD SOUTHFIELD) — Sentinel SecurityEvent (EID 4688)
let RmmAgents = dynamic(["\\agentmon.exe","\\kaseya.exe","\\screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","\\screenconnect.clientservice.exe","\\aexnsagent.exe","\\winagent.exe","\\saazapsc.exe"]);
let RmmPaths  = dynamic(["\\Kaseya\\","\\ConnectWise\\","\\N-able\\","\\SolarWinds\\MspAgent\\","\\Datto\\"]);
let KaseyaIOCs = dynamic(["kworking","agent.crt","mpsvc.dll"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where ParentProcessName has_any (RmmAgents) or ParentProcessName has_any (RmmPaths)
| where
      CommandLine has_any (KaseyaIOCs)
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\certutil.exe" and CommandLine has "-decode")
   or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "Set-MpPreference" and CommandLine has_any ("-DisableRealtimeMonitoring","-DisableIOAVProtection","-DisableBehaviorMonitoring","-DisableScriptScanning"))
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine has "copy" and CommandLine has_all ("certutil","\\Windows\\"))
   or (CommandLine has "MsMpEng.exe" and CommandLine !contains "\\Windows Defender\\")
   or (NewProcessName endswith "\\MsMpEng.exe" and NewProcessName !startswith "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\" and NewProcessName !startswith "C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentRMM = ParentProcessName,
          ChildBin  = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: This chain is the literal reproduction of the July 2021 Kaseya VSA supply-chain incident attributed to GOLD SOUTHFIELD/Pinchy Spider (Microsoft, Sophos, Huntress, CISA AA21-209A): the Kaseya AgentMon.exe pushed a malicious script that disabled Defender via Set-MpPreference, copied certutil to C:\Windows\cert.exe to evade certutil-name detections, base64-decoded C:\kworking\agent.crt into agent.exe
